England XI vs France: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup, injury latest for World Cup bronze final | The Standard

Sport| Football

# England XI vs France: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup, injury latest for World Cup bronze final

Thomas Tuchel expected to freshen up Three Lions side for third-place play-off

#### Allow Exco Player content

This content is provided by Exco Player and may use cookies or similar technologies. Please click 'Allow and Continue' below to load the content.

Allow and Continue

Dom Smith in Miami

2 minutes ago

COMMENTS

Thomas Tuchel is expected to make significant changes to his starting lineup when England face France in the World Cup 2026 bronze final on Saturday.

The Three Lions will look to finish the tournament in third place after losing their semi-final 2-1 to Argentina in heartbreaking late fashion in Atlanta on Wednesday.

Tuchel will alter his team back in sweltering Miami as he manages fatigue and looks to give minutes to players who have supported the squad but featured less than some of his regulars.

Captain Harry Kane and midfielder Jude Bellingham should both be keen to start the game, given they each have six goals and are st

Advertisement

Advertisement

Advertisement

Advertisement

TRENDING

A pair of 62s at Open

Caitlin Clark's career high

WC final: Spain vs. Argentina

MLB's top trade candidates

Story by

France XI vs England: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup, injury latest for World Cup bronze final

Didier Deschamps is expected to make changes for France's bronze final clash against England, though Kylian Mbappe could start in hope of claiming the World Cup 2026 Golden Boot.

Mbappe, who claimed the individual award four years ago in Qatar after scoring a hat-trick in the final loss to Argentina, is currently tied with Lionel Messi at the top of the standings.

Advertisement

Advertisement

Both players have eight goals at this World Cup, but Messi leads the way by virtue of one more assist (4) to Mbappe (3).

As a result, the Frenchman must register at least two goal involvement in Miami on Saturday- while hoping Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham do not get on the scoresheet - to have any chance of pipping Messi to the top scorer award.

Another incenti

news_items.title: England lineup vs. France: Predicted XI for World Cup 2026 third-placed playoff after Reece James injury blow - Sports Mole
news_items.text:
England lineup vs. France: Predicted XI for World Cup 2026 third-placed playoff after Reece James injury blow - Sports Mole

Menu

World Cup

France

Miami Stadium

England

# England lineup vs. France: Predicted XI for World Cup 2026 third-placed playoff after Reece James injury blow

By Ben Knapton| 16 Jul 2026 10:40

© Iconsport / Richard Callis/SPP/Alamy

England manager Thomas Tuchel has been dealt yet another right-back selection blow ahead of Saturday's World Cup 2026 third-placed playoff against France in Miami.

The Three Lions suffered a gut-wrenching but deserved 2-1 loss to Argentina in Wednesday's semi-final, in which the recently-recovered Reece James was forced off with a new apparent muscle issue in the last 10 minutes.

Tuchel was unsurprisingly not asked about James's condition in the immediate aftermath, but given the Chelsea man's history of fitness woes, an appearance at the Hard Rock Stadium is surely out of the question.

The Three Lions can once again call on Jarell Quansah after his two-game suspension ran its course, but the Bayer Leverkusen man should lose out to Nico O'Reilly, as Djed Spence returns to right-back after a standout showing on Wednesday.

T

# England lineup vs. France: Predicted XI for World Cup 2026 third-placed playoff after Reece James injury blow
...
England manager Thomas Tuchel has been dealt yet another right-back selection blow ahead of Saturday's World Cup 2026 third-placed playoff against France in Miami.
...
The Three Lions suffered a gut-wrenching but deserved 2-1 loss to Argentina in Wednesday's semi-final, in which the recently-recovered Reece James was forced off with a new apparent muscle issue in the last 10 minutes.
...
Tuchel was unsurprisingly not asked about James's condition in the immediate aftermath, but given the Chelsea man's history of fitness woes, an appearance at the Hard Rock Stadium is surely out of the question.
...
The Three Lions can once again call on Jarell Quansah after his two-game suspension ran its course, but the Bayer Leverkusen man should lose out to Nico O'Reilly, as Djed Spence returns to right-back after a standout showing on Wednesday.

Story by

England XI vs France: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup, injury latest for World Cup bronze final

Thomas Tuchel is expected to make significant changes to his starting lineup when England face France in the World Cup 2026 bronze final on Saturday.

The Three Lions will look to finish the tournament in third place after losing their semi-final 2-1 to Argentina in heartbreaking late fashion in Atlanta on Wednesday.

Advertisement

Advertisement

Tuchel will alter his team back in sweltering Miami as he manages fatigue and looks to give minutes to players who have supported the squad but featured less than some of his regulars.

Captain Harry Kane and midfielder Jude Bellingham should both be keen to start the game, given they each have six goals and are still chasing the World Cup Golden Boot. Kylian Mbappe and Lionel Messi currently lead the race with eight goals apiece.

Reece James went off injured in the second half o

France XI vs England: Confirmed team news, predicted lineup, injury latest for World Cup bronze final

Didier Deschamps is expected to make changes for France's bronze final clash against England, though Kylian Mbappe could start in hope of claiming the World Cup 2026 Golden Boot.

Mbappe, who claimed the individual award four years ago in Qatar after scoring a hat-trick in the final loss to Argentina, is currently tied with Lionel Messi at the top of the standings.

Advertisement

Advertisement

Both players have eight goals at this World Cup, but Messi leads the way by virtue of one more assist (4) to Mbappe (3).

As a result, the Frenchman must register at least two goal involvement in Miami on Saturday- while hoping Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham do not get on the scoresheet - to have any chance of pipping Messi to the top scorer award.

# England lineup vs. France: Predicted XI for World Cup 2026 third-placed playoff after Reece James injury blow

By Ben Knapton| 16 Jul 2026 10:40

© Iconsport / Richard Callis/SPP/Alamy

England manager Thomas Tuchel has been dealt yet another right-back selection blow ahead of Saturday's World Cup 2026 third-placed playoff against France in Miami.

The Three Lions suffered a gut-wrenching but deserved 2-1 loss to Argentina in Wednesday's semi-final, in which the recently-recovered Reece James was forced off with a new apparent muscle issue in the last 10 minutes.

Tuchel was unsurprisingly not asked about James's condition in the immediate aftermath, but given the Chelsea man's history of fitness woes, an appearance at the Hard Rock Stadium is surely out of the question.

The Three Lions can once again call on Jarell Quansah after his two-game suspension ran its course, but the Bayer Leverkusen man should lose out to Nico O'Reilly, as Djed Spence returns to right-back after a standout showing on Wednesday.
